title: 建立 Android 線上編譯器程式
content:
hello, wanna type and compile code in your app on your own device? In this article, we will learn how to create an online compiler application using CodeX API, a powerful API which provides functions to compile and execute program codes.

Here is the documentaion of CodeX API .

特别注意

CodeX API is still in early stages development and not recommended to be used in production. Plus, this is a simple online compiler application using CodeX API only. Therefore, there won’t be advanced features of compilation.

However, there’s a good new that CodeX API supports program input e.g. Java Scanner, which helps us write simple programs on mobile.

Manifest Configuration

Set permission

Since we are gonna build online compiler application, it requires Internet and network access. Therefore, we have to add these codes in AndroidManifest.xml to grant permissions for the application.

<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE" />

Allow HTTP URLs access

Starting from Android 9 (API level 28) and above, it disallows HTTP URLs by default. It will throw error messages informing that cleartext HTTP traffic is not permitted if there are programs that access HTTP URLs.

If the URL can’t be really changed and connected from HTTP to HTTPS, while you want to use the HTTP URL to contiune building your project.

There are two solutions of resolving HTTP traffic errors:

Solution 1 - Cleartext Traffic

In AndroidManifest.xml, set android:usesCleartextTraffic property to true.

<application
    android:usesCleartextTraffic="true">
</application>

Solution 2 - Allow connections to specific site

I used this method before, and I still recommend using it. The second way is to allow connections to specific sites.

Unlike the solution one, we need to create a new XML file named network_security_config.xml in /res/xml/.

<network-security-config>
<domain-config cleartextTrafficPermitted="true">
    <domain includeSubdomains="true">...</domain>
</domain-config>
</network-security-config>

The ... to be replaced is your desired HTTP URL. Once you put it inside the <domain>, it can be permitted and be accessed.

小提示

For example, if I want to enable this HTTP URL: bluewhaleyt.ddns.net, then I will do the following like this.

<network-security-config>
<domain-config cleartextTrafficPermitted="true">
    <domain includeSubdomains="true">bluewhaleyt.ddns.net</domain>
</domain-config>
</network-security-config>

After doing this, you need to set android:networkSecurityConfig in the AndroidManifest.xml.

<application
    android:networkSecurityConfig="@xml/network_security_config">
</application>

Done, it’s now ready to be accessed.

小提示

Both solutions are great. To make it simple, I prefer Solution 1, which is much easier.

Create Layout

In this project, we will use Sora Editor as a code editor instead of using EditText. With this, we can type codes with syntax highlighting.
However, the guide of using Sora Editor will not be written here. You can read this article for the details.

Here are dependencies of Sora Editor and the layout in case you are familiar with using them.

implementation(platform("io.github.Rosemoe.sora-editor:bom:0.20.2"))
implementation("io.github.Rosemoe.sora-editor:editor")
implementation("io.github.Rosemoe.sora-editor:language-textmate")
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<LinearLayout
    xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    xmlns:app="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res-auto"
    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="match_parent"
    android:orientation="vertical">

    <io.github.rosemoe.sora.widget.CodeEditor
        android:id="@+id/editor"
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="match_parent" />

</LinearLayout>

RequestNetwork Java Files

Sketchware Pro provides the utils of network request components, RequestNetwork.java and RequestNetworkController.java. These are the useful utility program files helping us to fetch data from URL easily.

We can simply bring these files to our project in case you don’t have them.

RequestNetwork.java 1 KB
RequestNetworkController.java 7 KB

Besides, we need to add okhttp dependency.

implementation(platform("com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp-bom:4.10.0"))
implementation("com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp")

Use CodeX API

As aforementioned, we will build an online compiler application with CodeX API.

This is the URL that the API is located in: 

https://api.codex.jaagrav.in

We will use this API to compile and execute program codes.

Create a Compiler class

In order to make our project be clean, we will create a new Java class Compiler.java to do it so.

Your Compiler.java should be like this:

public class Compiler {
    ...
}

In this class, there will be two methods, init() and compile(), both of them must be public since they will be called in MainActivity.java later.

Define Variables

Now, initialize the following variables.

private static String url = "https://api.codex.jaagrav.in";

private static HashMap<String, Object> map = new HashMap<>();
private static HashMap<String, Object> data = new HashMap<>();

private static RequestNetwork requestNetwork;
private static RequestNetwork.RequestListener requestListener;

private static ModernDialog.Builder dialog;
private static AlertDialog progressDialog;

ModernDialog.Builder belongs to ModernDialog library which is created by myself. If you want to use this library, you can read this article.

小提示

Instead of using ModernDialog, you can either use AlertDialog or MaterialAlertDialogBuilder.

Initialize RequestNetwork

To start a new instance of RequestNetwork, add the code below.

RequestNetwork requestNetwork = new RequestNetwork(this);

We will create a new method init() to do the initialization stuffs.

public static void init(Activity activity) {
    requestNetwork = new RequestNetwork(activity);
}

Use RequestListener

This is the basic start of RequestListener.

RequestListener requestListener = new RequestNetwork.RequestListener() {
    @Override
    public void onResponse(String tag, String response, HashMap<String, Object> responseHeaders) {
        
    }

    @Override
    public void onErrorResponse(String tag, String message) {

    }
};

小提示

onResponse() is to fetch data from URLs if there’s no errors occuring.
onErrorResponse() is to throw errors during the issues of fetching data from URLs.

Loading dialog

In both onResponse() and onErrorResponse() methods, we need to define a progress dialog that tells users that the data from URLs is fetching, which takes time to fetch.

public static void compile(Activity activity) {
    ...
}

Create a new method compile() and add the progress dialog.

注意

In this article, Progress Dialog will not be detailed, see article.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<LinearLayout xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="match_parent">
    <LinearLayout
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content"
        android:orientation="horizontal"
        android:padding="20dp">
        <ProgressBar
            android:id="@+id/pbLoading"
            style="?android:attr/progressBarStyle"
            android:layout_width="wrap_content"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:layout_marginLeft="10dp"
            android:layout_marginRight="20dp" />
        <TextView
            android:id="@+id/tvText"
            android:layout_width="wrap_content"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:layout_gravity="center"
            android:text="TextView" />
    </LinearLayout>
</LinearLayout>
public static void compile(Activity activity) {
    View view = activity.getLayoutInflater().inflate(R.layout.dialog_progress, null);
    TextView tv = view.findViewById(R.id.tvText);
    tv.setText("Compiling, please wait ...");

    progressDialog = new MaterialAlertDialogBuilder(activity).create();
    progressDialog.setView(view);
    progressDialog.setCancelable(false);
    progressDialog.show();
}

When there are responses, dismiss the progress dialog.

@Override
public void onResponse(String tag, String response, HashMap<String, Object> responseHeaders) {
    progressDialog.dismiss();
}

@Override
public void onErrorResponse(String tag, String message) {
    progressDialog.dismiss();
}

Connection Error Alert

Once again, this is an online compiler which requires Internet connection. We need to alert users activating it before starting to code.

To do this, we need to use onErrorResponse() method.

@Override
public void onErrorResponse(String tag, String message) {
    progressDialog.dismiss();

    dialog.setTitle("No Internet");
    dialog.setMessage(message);
}

Fetch data

We will use Gson to stringify JSON, add dependency:

implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.10'

To put response as a part of JSON to data using Gson:

data = new Gson().fromJson(response, new TypeToken<HashMap<String, Object>>(){}.getType());

Therefore, we have the following code.

@Override
public void onResponse(String tag, String response, HashMap<String, Object> responseHeaders) {
    progressDialog.dismiss();

    data = new Gson().fromJson(response, new TypeToken<HashMap<String, Object>>(){}.getType());
}

Compilation

CodeX API compilation needs requirement below:

  1. code - the code to be compiled
  2. language - the programming language to be used
  3. input - the execution of program input

Here’s how to set the configuration.

map.put("code", strCode);
map.put("language", strLang);
map.put("input", strInput);

requestNetwork.setParams(map, RequestNetworkController.REQUEST_BODY);

We are going to extend the compile() method with three more parameters: text, lang, and input.

public static void compile(Activity activity, String text, String lang, String input) {

    map = new HashMap<>();
    map.put("code", text);
    map.put("language", lang);
    map.put("input", input);

    requestNetwork.setParams(map, RequestNetworkController.REQUEST_BODY);
}

After the setting is done, starts the RequestNetwork.

requestNetwork.startRequestNetwork(RequestNetworkController.POST, url, "api", requestListener);

小提示

url is "https://api.codex.jaagrav.in", which has already been defined. If you do not, add this code.

private static String url = "https://api.codex.jaagrav.in";

During the compilation, there will be one of two results, Compilation Success and Compilation Failed.

Compilation Success

We will declare it as a success situation if there’s no error response. Then, display the compilation output.

if (data.get("error").toString().equals("")) {
    dialog.setTitle("Compilation output");
    dialog.setMessage(data.get("output").toString());
}

小提示

data.get("error") is to fetch error responses if there’s any.
data.get("output") is to fetch the output of compilation if there’s no error responses.

In order to check and display as a String, we need to set toString() for them.

Compilation Failed

The compilation failed means there’s error response during the compilation. If there are errors, display them.

else {
    dialog.setTitle("Compilation error");
    dialog.setMessage(data.get("error").toString());
}

注意

Don’t forget to show the dialog after the if statement.

dialog.show();

Following instructions above, your code should be like below:

@Override
public void onResponse(String tag, String response, HashMap<String, Object> responseHeaders) {
    progressDialog.dismiss();
    data = new Gson().fromJson(response, new TypeToken<HashMap<String, Object>>(){}.getType());

    if (data.get("error").toString().equals("")) {
        dialog.setTitle("Compilation output");
        dialog.setMessage(data.get("output").toString());
    }
    else {
        dialog.setTitle("Compilation error");
        dialog.setMessage(data.get("error").toString());
    }

    dialog.show();
}

Call Compiler class

We’re almost there, we’re now in the step of calling methods from Compiler class.

Your MainActivity.java should be like this.

public class MainActivity extends AppCompatActivity {
    private ActivityMainBinding binding;

    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
        binding = ActivityMainBinding.inflate(getLayoutInflater());
        setContentView(binding.getRoot());
    }
}

注意

View Binding - binding is not required to be used in this project. However, it is a good way to get rid of using findViewById().

For instance:

// get TextView's text using findViewById()
String tv = findViewById(R.id.tv);
String text = tv.getText().toString();

// get TextView's text using view binding
String text = binding.tv.getText().toString();

Therefore, using view binding has several advantages:

  1. Supports null safety. There won’t be Attempt to invoke virtual method on a null object reference errors since the components are binded in view binding. It also prevents non-existent views or components being called.
  2. Reduces boileplate code, since findViewById() requires to be defined again if it is being used again, which takes too many places of the entire program and wastes on the operations. If it comes to view binding, it literally eliminates these problems.
  3. Facilitates type safety, since the binding class that’s generated matches the views declared in the layout file.

To compile, call compile() from the Compiler class.

Compiler.compile(this, strCode, strLang, strInput);

Handle input execution

Now, we need to implement the compilation of input. We will make a new dialog with its custom view in MainActivity.java. Again, ModernDialog is used, you can use others.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<LinearLayout xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="match_parent"
    android:orientation="vertical"
    android:paddingLeft="10dp"
    android:paddingRight="10dp">
    <com.google.android.material.textfield.TextInputLayout
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content">
        <EditText
            android:id="@+id/et"
            android:layout_width="match_parent"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:hint="Input" />
    </com.google.android.material.textfield.TextInputLayout>
</LinearLayout>
@Override
protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
    ...
    dialog = new ModernDialog.Builder(this)
            .setDialogStyle(ModernDialog.DIALOG_STYLE_BOTTOM_SHEET)
            .setView(R.layout.dialog_edittext)
            .setTitle("Leave it empty if no inputs is needed.")
            .setPositiveButton(null, v -> {
                EditText et = dialog.dialogBS.findViewById(R.id.et);
                String input = et.getText().toString();
                Compiler.compile(this, binding.editor.getText().toString(), "java", input);
            })
            .show();
}

Lastly, call init() from the Compiler class to initialize the RequestNetwork.

Compiler.init(this);

特别注意

The initialization - init() should be executed before the compilation compile(). Otherwise, the program will never work and show onResponse() or onErrorResponse() with Attempt to invoke interface method on a null object reference.

To make sure you don’t get lost, here’s the code:

public class MainActivity extends AppCompatActivity {
    private ActivityMainBinding binding;

    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
        binding = ActivityMainBinding.inflate(getLayoutInflater());
        setContentView(binding.getRoot());

        Compiler.init(this);
    }
}

You may ask, where does compile() code? It should be executed on a button click or else.

Here’s an example of defining a Button and executing its click event.

<Button
    android:id="@+id/btn"
    android:layout_width="wrap_content"
    android:layout_height="wrap_content"
    android:text="Compile" />
binding.btn.setOnClickListener(v -> {
    dialog = new ModernDialog.Builder(this)
            .setDialogStyle(ModernDialog.DIALOG_STYLE_BOTTOM_SHEET)
            .setView(R.layout.dialog_edittext)
            .setTitle("Leave it empty if no inputs is needed.")
            .setPositiveButton(null, v -> {
                EditText et = dialog.dialogBS.findViewById(R.id.et);
                String input = et.getText().toString();
                Compiler.compile(this, binding.editor.getText().toString(), "java", input);
            })
            .show();
})

Full source code

Here’s the full source code.

注意

Due to the source code below was written before this article is created, some of the code may not match with this article’s content codes. Plus, replace the package name to yours.

// ==== [ Gson ] ====
implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.10'

// ==== [ Okhttp ] ====
implementation(platform("com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp-bom:4.10.0"))
implementation("com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp")

// ==== [ Sora Editor ] =====
implementation(platform("io.github.Rosemoe.sora-editor:bom:0.20.2"))
implementation("io.github.Rosemoe.sora-editor:editor")
implementation("io.github.Rosemoe.sora-editor:language-textmate")

// === [ Modern Dialog ] ====
implementation 'com.github.BlueWhaleYT:ModernDialog:1.0.2'

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE" />

    <application
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:usesCleartextTraffic="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:theme="@style/Theme.JavaIDE"
        tools:targetApi="31">
        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:hardwareAccelerated="true"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>

            <meta-data
                android:name="android.app.lib_name"
                android:value="" />
        </activity>

        <uses-library
            android:name="org.apache.http.legacy"
            android:required="false" />
    </application>

</manifest>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<LinearLayout
    xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    xmlns:app="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res-auto"
    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="match_parent"
    tools:context=".MainActivity"
    android:orientation="vertical">

    <io.github.rosemoe.sora.widget.CodeEditor
        android:id="@+id/editor"
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="match_parent" />

</LinearLayout>

package com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.compiler;

import android.app.Activity;
import android.app.ProgressDialog;
import android.content.Context;
import android.view.View;
import android.widget.TextView;
import android.widget.Toast;

import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog;

import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.App;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.R;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.util.network.RequestNetwork;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.util.network.RequestNetworkController;
import com.bluewhaleyt.moderndialog.ModernDialog;
import com.google.android.material.dialog.MaterialAlertDialogBuilder;
import com.google.gson.Gson;
import com.google.gson.reflect.TypeToken;

import java.util.HashMap;

import io.github.rosemoe.sora.widget.CodeEditor;

public class Compiler {

    private static String url = "https://api.codex.jaagrav.in";

    private static HashMap<String, Object> map = new HashMap<>();
    private static HashMap<String, Object> data = new HashMap<>();

    private static RequestNetwork requestNetwork;
    private static RequestNetwork.RequestListener requestListener;

    private static ModernDialog.Builder dialog;
    private static AlertDialog progressDialog;

    public static void init(Activity activity) {

        requestNetwork = new RequestNetwork(activity);
        dialog = new ModernDialog.Builder(activity)
                .setDialogStyle(ModernDialog.DIALOG_STYLE_BOTTOM_SHEET)
                .setTitleTextAlignment(ModernDialog.ALIGNMENT_LEFT)
                .setMessageTextAlignment(ModernDialog.ALIGNMENT_LEFT)
                .setPositiveButton(false)
                .setNegativeButton(false)
                .setCancelable(true, true);

        requestListener = new RequestNetwork.RequestListener() {
            @Override
            public void onResponse(String tag, String response, HashMap<String, Object> responseHeaders) {
                progressDialog.dismiss();
                data = new Gson().fromJson(response, new TypeToken<HashMap<String, Object>>(){}.getType());

                if (data.get("error").toString().equals("")) {
                    dialog.setTitle("Compilation output");
                    dialog.setMessage(data.get("output").toString());
                    dialog.setButtonsDisabled(true);
                }
                else {
                    dialog.setAnimationLoop(true);
                    dialog.setAnimation("https://assets6.lottiefiles.com/temp/lf20_QYm9j9.json");
                    dialog.setTitle("Compilation error");
                    dialog.setMessage(data.get("error").toString());
                    dialog.setButtonsDisabled(true);
                }

                dialog.show();
            }

            @Override
            public void onErrorResponse(String tag, String message) {
                progressDialog.dismiss();

                dialog.setAnimationLoop(true);
                dialog.setAnimation("https://assets4.lottiefiles.com/packages/lf20_lvuoopxx.json");
                dialog.setTitle("No connection");
                dialog.setMessage("Message:\n" + message);
                dialog.setButtonsDisabled(true);
                dialog.show();
            }
        };

    }

    public static void compile(Activity activity, String text, String lang, String input) {

        map = new HashMap<>();
        map.put("code", text);
        map.put("language", lang);
        map.put("input", input);

        requestNetwork.setParams(map, RequestNetworkController.REQUEST_BODY);
        requestNetwork.startRequestNetwork(RequestNetworkController.POST, url, "api", requestListener);

        View view = activity.getLayoutInflater().inflate(R.layout.dialog_progress, null);
        TextView tv = view.findViewById(R.id.tvText);
        tv.setText("Please wait ..");

        progressDialog = new MaterialAlertDialogBuilder(activity).create();
        progressDialog.setView(view);
        progressDialog.setCancelable(false);
        progressDialog.show();

    }

}

package com.bluewhaleyt.javaide;

import androidx.annotation.NonNull;
import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity;

import android.app.ProgressDialog;
import android.os.Bundle;
import android.view.Menu;
import android.view.MenuItem;
import android.view.View;
import android.widget.ArrayAdapter;
import android.widget.EditText;

import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.compiler.Compiler;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.databinding.ActivityMainBinding;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.editor.Editor;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.textmate.syntaxhighlight.SyntaxHighlighter;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.util.AssetsFileLoader;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.util.network.RequestNetwork;
import com.bluewhaleyt.javaide.util.network.RequestNetworkController;
import com.bluewhaleyt.moderndialog.ModernDialog;
import com.google.gson.Gson;
import com.google.gson.reflect.TypeToken;

import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.HashMap;

public class MainActivity extends AppCompatActivity {

    private ActivityMainBinding binding;
    private ModernDialog dialog;

    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
        binding = ActivityMainBinding.inflate(getLayoutInflater());
        setContentView(binding.getRoot());
        initialize();
    }

    public void initialize() {

        // initialize code editor
        Editor.initEditor(this, binding.editor);

        // initialize textmate configuration
        SyntaxHighlighter.init(this, binding.editor);

        // initialize network request
        Compiler.init(this);

    }

    @Override
    public boolean onCreateOptionsMenu(Menu menu) {
        getMenuInflater().inflate(R.menu.menu, menu);
        return super.onCreateOptionsMenu(menu);
    }

    @Override
    public boolean onOptionsItemSelected(@NonNull MenuItem item) {

        switch (item.getItemId()) {
            case R.id.compile:
                compile();
                break;
            case R.id.template:
                binding.editor.setText(AssetsFileLoader.getFileContent(this, "preset/Main.java"));
                break;
        }

        return super.onOptionsItemSelected(item);
    }

    public void compile() {
        dialog = new ModernDialog.Builder(this)
                .setDialogStyle(ModernDialog.DIALOG_STYLE_BOTTOM_SHEET)
                .setView(R.layout.dialog_edittext)
                .setTitle("Leave it empty if no inputs is needed.")
                .setPositiveButton(null, v -> {
                    EditText et = dialog.dialogBS.findViewById(R.id.et);
                    var input = et.getText().toString();
                    Compiler.compile(this, binding.editor.getText().toString(), "java", input);
                })
                .show();
    }

}
text: hello, wanna type and compile code in your app on your own device? In this article, we will learn how to create an online compiler applicati...
toc:
1. Manifest Configuration
    1. Set permission
    2. Allow HTTP URLs access
      1. Solution 1 - Cleartext Traffic
      2. Solution 2 - Allow connections to specific site
  2. Create Layout
  3. RequestNetwork Java Files
  4. Use CodeX API
    1. Create a Compiler class
    2. Define Variables
    3. Initialize RequestNetwork
    4. Use RequestListener
    5. Loading dialog
    6. Connection Error Alert
    7. Fetch data
    8. Compilation
      1. Compilation Success
      2. Compilation Failed
    9. Call Compiler class
      1. Handle input execution
  5. Full source code

author.name: BlueWhaleYT
author.description: Everything is possible, as long as you are powerful.

next_post.title: VSCode 自訂程式碼片段
next_post.text: 哈囉，歡迎來到我的網誌。 有沒有想過自己在編寫程式碼時，能夠借助自動完成選單來完成一整塊的程式碼？在 VSCode 軟件，它能夠支援自訂程式碼片段，使你能夠在輸入關鍵字的前綴後，便能插入一整塊程式碼！大大提升你編程的效率。 在這篇文章，我們將會學到如何新增自己的程式碼片段。 為編...

author.name: BlueWhaleYT
author.description: Everything is possible, as long as you are powerful.
